gleam 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Calculate Energy for Fibre Production

Source: R/energy_requirements_core_model.R
calc_net_energy_fibre.Rd

Computes the energy requirement for fibre production (MJ/head/day), defined as the energy needed to produce animal fibres such as wool or hair. This component follows the IPCC Tier 2 partitioning approach and is applied only to fibre-producing species and relevant cohorts, which are assumed by the model to be = ("FA", "FS", "MA", "MS").

calc_net_energy_fibre(animal, cohort, fibre_prod)

Arguments

animal

Character. Code identifying the livestock species. Supported values include:

  • PGS: pigs

  • CML: camels

  • CTL: cattle

  • BFL: buffalo

  • SHP: sheep

  • GTS: goats

cohort

Character. Sex- and age-specific cohort code describing the production stage of the animals. Supported values include:

  • FA: adult females (from age at first parturition)

  • FS: sub-adult females (from weaning to age at first parturition)

  • FJ: juvenile females (from birth to weaning)

  • MA: adult males (from age at first breeding)

  • MS: sub-adult males (from weaning to age at first breeding)

  • MJ: juvenile males (from birth to weaning)

fibre_prod

Numeric. Annual fibre production yield (kg/head/year).

Value

Numeric. Energy required for the synthesis of fibre for SHP, GTS and CML. Assumed to be 0 for other species. (MJ/head/day). Expressed as net energy for CTL, BFL, SHP, GTS and as metabolizable energy for CML and PGS (MJ/head/day).

Details

  • SHP and GTS - (AFRC 1995); IPCC (2006, 2019):

    For sheep and goats, fibre production energy is calculated assuming a fixed net energy cost of \(24\) MJ per kilogram of fibre produced. Annual fibre production is converted to a daily requirement as:

    $$ energy\_fibre = \frac{24 \times fibre\_prod}{365} $$

    where:

    • \(fibre\_prod\) is annual fibre production (kg head\(^{-1}\) year\(^{-1}\)),

    • \(24\) is the net energy requirement per kilogram of fibre (MJ kg fibre\(^{-1}\)).

  • CML - (AFRC, 1998; Cannas et al, 2007):

    For camels, energy requirements for fibre production are first calculated on a net energy (NE) basis and then converted to metabolizable energy (ME) using a net-to-metabolizable energy efficiency coefficient.

    Fibre energy requirements are calculated as:

    $$ energy\_fibre = \frac{24}{0.43} \times \frac{fibre\_prod}{365} $$

    where:

    • \(24\) is the net energy requirement per kilogram of fibre (MJ kg fibre\(^{-1}\)),

    • \(0.43\) is the efficiency of conversion from metabolizable energy to net energy for fibre production (dimensionless fraction),

    • fibre_prod is the annual fibre production per animal (kg head\(^{-1}\) year\(^{-1}\)),

    • division by \(365\) converts annual fibre production to a daily basis.

    The efficiency coefficient of 0.43 is adopted by analogy with goats, assuming a dietary metabolizability of approximately 0.55, following AFRC guidance and subsequent synthesis by Cannas et al. (2007).

  • Other species Fibre production energy is assumed to be zero for cattle, buffalo, pigs, and poultry.

References

AFRC (1998) The Nutrition of Goats. Wallingford: CAB International. Animut G., Puchala R., Goetsch A.L., Patra A.K., Sahlu T., Varel V.H., Wells J.

AFRC (1995). Energy and Protein Requirements of Ruminants. CAB International, Wallingford, UK.

Cannas, A., Atzori, A. S., Boe, F., & Teixeira, I. (2007). Energy and protein requirements of goats. In: Dairy sheep nutrition (pp. 31-49). CAB International, Wallingford, UK.

IPCC. (2019). 2019 Refinement to the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories. Chapter 10: Emissions from Livestock and Manure Management, Equation 10.12.

IPCC. (2006). 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories. Chapter 10: Emissions from Livestock and Manure Management, Equation 10.12.

Contents

Developed by Ahmed Jou, Yassine Elaouni, Dominik Wisser, Lydia Lanzoni, Giuseppe Tempio, Federal Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Regional Identity of Germany BMELH.
